proof.title: Short explanation clip
proof.body: I explained one idea in 40 seconds.

input.reflectionText: I was nervous but clearer on the second try.

ideal_output.validation: It makes sense that a short clip felt awkward.
ideal_output.what_practiced: You practiced explaining one idea with less extra setup.
ideal_output.next_step: Try one more take with a slower first sentence.

proof.title: Five minute task
proof.body: I opened the document and wrote the first heading.

input.reflectionText: It was tiny, but I started.

ideal_output.validation: Starting counts because it made the task visible.
ideal_output.what_practiced: You practiced lowering the start cost.
ideal_output.next_step: Add one bullet under the heading.

proof.title: Better question
proof.body: I changed 'why am I stuck' to 'what would unblock the next step'.

input.reflectionText: The new question felt more useful.

ideal_output.validation: That change made the question easier to answer.
ideal_output.what_practiced: You practiced turning pressure into a clear request.
ideal_output.next_step: Use the same question format once today.

proof.title: Preference sentence
proof.body: I said I prefer the morning slot because I focus better then.

input.reflectionText: I did not apologize after it.

ideal_output.validation: That is a concrete practice rep.
ideal_output.what_practiced: You practiced naming a preference plainly.
ideal_output.next_step: Use the same structure for one low-stakes choice.

proof.title: Feedback rewrite
proof.body: I changed 'good job' into one observation and one next step.

ideal_output.validation: Specific feedback gives the other person something to use.
ideal_output.what_practiced: You practiced responding to the work, not judging the person.
ideal_output.next_step: Add one example from the proof next time.

proof.title: Boundary draft
proof.body: I wrote: I cannot take this on today, but I can look Friday.

ideal_output.validation: The draft is clear and respectful.
ideal_output.what_practiced: You practiced a boundary with a concrete alternative.
ideal_output.next_step: Read it once and remove any extra apology.

proof.title: Decision map
proof.body: I wrote two options and one tradeoff for each.

ideal_output.validation: The map made the decision easier to inspect.
ideal_output.what_practiced: You practiced separating options from assumptions.
ideal_output.next_step: Circle the tradeoff you can accept today.

proof.title: Restart note
proof.body: I missed two days and did the smallest version today.

input.reflectionText: I did not make it a big reset.

ideal_output.validation: A small restart is still useful evidence.
ideal_output.what_practiced: You practiced returning without turning it into a judgment.

proof.title: Peer note
proof.body: I sent one clear note about what worked and one next step.

input.reflectionText: I stayed inside what they asked for.

ideal_output.validation: Staying inside the request makes feedback safer.
ideal_output.what_practiced: You practiced useful contribution with restraint.
ideal_output.next_step: Keep the same two-part structure next time.

proof.title: Main point first
proof.body: I moved my main point to the first sentence.

input.reflectionText: The message was easier to read.

ideal_output.validation: That edit made the message easier to scan.
ideal_output.what_practiced: You practiced making the next reader's job easier.
ideal_output.next_step: Use the same first-sentence check on one more message.
